Ms Larissa and Ms. Lisa have attended and they have given a small brief about what they are looking for.

Kaufland is currently looking for peanut butter for which the peanuts are sourced from the US. They have asked if the supplier can source peanuts from the US and then produce peanut butter. The supplier has refused, saying that he does produce peanut butter from peanuts sourced from India, and he will be able to offer that product to Kaufland. However, Kaufland has replied that they have a mandate that the peanuts need to be sourced from the US for their private label peanut butter. 

Kaufland has also informed that the tender for regular Peanut Butter - Creamy and crunchy tender will be open from mid of May end of June. But they are not sure if the US mandate will be removed by then.

column54:
Unnamed: 8: February 2025

Initial introduction of supplier Excel (Arshad Group) by Geotex — no mention of Lidl buyer at this stage.

March 2025

EEC noticed Arshad Group used the Lidl logo in their brochure.

EEC emailed Geotex to verify the supplier’s relationship with Lidl.

Geotex responded that Arshad received inquiries from Lidl years ago but no orders were placed. Arshad is open to working with Lidl through Elan Export.

Supplier information shared with the Lidl buyer.

Buyer confirmed their Asia team has been briefed to investigate as per standard procedure.

Buyer reconfirmed that the Asia team will handle the standard investigation.

Lidl Pakistan team contacted Mr. Faisal directly (without Elan/Geotex involvement).

EEC informed Geotex about this unauthorized contact.

Geotex clarified that Ms. Namrah (Lidl) reached out to Faisal’s team instead of Mr. Shoaib Azhar (official contact).

EEC reconfirmed that Shoaib Azhar’s details were the ones shared with Lidl, not Faisal’s.

Arshad Group informed EEC that Mr. Muhammad Ali from Lidl visited their factory for evaluation.

Arshad mentioned an earlier Lidl Karachi office visit in Oct 2024 without follow-up.

Arshad requested alignment of communication and proposed that all future processes go through Elan Export.

Suggested EEC send an official confirmation email to both parties.

April 2025

EEC held a meeting with Geotex; Geotex agreed to collect all relevant details from Arshad and keep EEC updated.

Buyer update:

Lidl Pakistan team visited Arshad’s factory.

Factory setup found acceptable for further exploration with some improvement areas.

Lidl to proceed with trial quotations and sampling to assess competitiveness.

Feedback to follow after initial results.

May 2025

Email from Johannes (Lidl) — Asia office still awaiting samples and quotations, requested urgent update.

Email from Asad: Arshad Group fully aligned and committed to working through Elan/Geotex.

EEC requested Asad to share communication threads between Lidl and Arshad for transparency.

June 2025

Price inquiry had been sent on 11 April 2025; communication ongoing between supplier and Lidl, but Elan not in CC.

On this date, Pakistan team included Elan in CC for reworked pricing discussions.

Lidl team followed up for better pricing; email forwarded to Asad.

Supplier’s revised quotation sent to buyer:

Curtains (135x254 cm, 2 pcs): Revised price $5.85 FOB, 50% white / 50% beige, recycled polyester used.

Duvets: No change in pricing.

Mr. Naved shared quotation to Lidl.

Email from Shoaib Azhar (Arshad Group) to Naved, stating he would discuss pricing with Mr. Faisal and involve Mr. Rashid for rework.

Signed Norma Sign Addendum received.

July 2025

Lidl informed Arshad that onboarding will not proceed — prices are higher than existing suppliers.

Arshad responded — Mr. Rashid will share improved pricing and expressed willingness for future opportunities.

Lidl Pakistan team (Mr. Talal Saud) sent trial quotation sheet (old + new products) to Mr. Shoaib Azhar.

Arshad shared socks stock Excel file, tagging Mr. Tariq.

Mr. Rashid (Arshad) shared revised quotation — two products (pillowcase & bed linen) matched Lidl’s target price.

Email from Peter Steger (Lidl Singapore):

Prices remain higher than existing vendors.

Even with partial matches, overall group pricing not competitive (e.g., Noble Renforce IANs 494983–494985).

Decision: Onboarding not to continue.

EEC emailed Johannes and Peter for latest updates.

Peter Steger reconfirmed that supplier’s prices remain too high, and onboarding will not proceed. | WEEK 38: Column 1 | WEEK 39: Column 2 | WEEK 40: Column 3 | WEEK 41: Column 4

meeting points
-- supplier worked with Lidl in 2019-2020 through wholsaler and in bulk packaging 
-Supplier won't be able to match up prices with Lidl target earlier in 2019-2020
--also salt meansuring was an issue earlier as supplier not use to it
--supplier always pay commission 0.05-0.10 cents per kg to other agents

15.10.24 --Prices received from supplier 

--EZ PEEL size 37-45 with ASC Certification

--Presentation: retail bag

--PRICE: $11.07 CIF Rotterdam

--Price valid until October 31th

--Supplier may agree on a 3-month price program, starting shipments in late December/early January 2025. Longer than that, lets say a 6 month program, supplier would have to check internally if its possible. 

--Regarding samples, Supplier can arrange samples but let us know if customer can pay for airfreight, supplier pay all the rest. There is a risk in shipping airfreight samples as explained.

--Payment terms but please note that Supplier  work with an advance payment of 20% and balance CAD at sight against copy of BL and resto of shipping docs sent by email.

--Supplier ship directly to customers so they don't have prodct in warehouses in Europe.  Option here is send pictures and perhaps samples in a container to another customer in Germany or near by for buyer to pick up and deliver.

2. Supplier wanted to know the target price? So that they can check what they can do.


17.10.24 -- email reply to supplier 

1) It would be best to discuss samples directly with the buyer. 

However, it’s important to note that when the buyer requests samples, please don't say no to sending them. But instead, just give them different options for getting samples. You can explain that due to lack of direct flight available the samples don’t reach in good condition as experienced in the past. 

Here are a few suggestions you can give them: 

1)    Send them to the Amsterdam office or Madrid office for evaluation if possible. 
2)    Send to a nearby location for the Buyer to pick up or get delivered.
3)    Send samples in a container via another customer in Germany.

2) Target price US$ 6 - US$ 7

18.10.24 -- Supplier reply  "We offered $11.00/kg and your target price is $6-$7 USD, far far from our current price so I think there is no need to even talk about samples"

we respond to the supplier If there will be any change in the prices later on we will surely keep you posted.

Seasonal Shipment Feasibility:
Long-voyage shipments are undertaken only during the fresh harvest season, typically from January to April / early May, as onions harvested during this period are strong and suitable for extended transit.

Off-Season Limitation:
Outside this harvest window, Indian onions are less robust and not suitable for long voyages, which may affect shipment feasibility.

White Onion Harvest:
In addition to Red and Pink onions, we also supply White onions (sizes 50–70 mm & 60–80 mm). The harvest for White onions also begins in January.

R&D ongoing with Skippy samples from Europe.

December 2024

Supplier finalizing sample development; planned shipment within the week.

January 2025

Buyer: send only if benchmark quality achieved.

Supplier asked about peanut butter origin already selling in Lidl stores.

February 2025

Samples shipped via DHL (AWB 2364932614).

March 2025

Follow-up reminders and supplier feedback email sent.

Buyer feedback — taste good, but consistency still not matching Skippy; asked for improvement.

Supplier confirmed ongoing trials and updates soon.

May 2025

Buyer follow-up; supplier replied they’re still working on sample improvements.

June 2025

Samples under customs clearance.

Buyer provided documents to courier.

Supplier contacted Stefan for customs clearance.

Samples delivered.

Awaiting buyer feedback. 

Follow-up email for feedback.

Buyer rejected samples — texture too liquid, taste lacks roasted flavor, color differs from Skippy.

Supplier to inspect rejected samples and respond.
